_meta.description: Travala master city roster, organized Continent > Country > City. 120 cities total across all 6 inhabited continents.

entry.visa_note: Complex and nationality-dependent: some nationalities get 30-day visa-free entry, others qualify for 10-day or 240-hour visa-free transit if continuing to a third country, others need a full visa or Shenzhen-specific 5-day Visa on Arrival. Rules vary significantly — verify carefully by nationality and itinerary before travel.

transport.payment_card: Shenzhen Tong card or WeChat Pay/Alipay QR (increasingly the dominant payment method city-wide)
main_systems: Shenzhen Metro (17 lines, one of the world's largest systems)
transport.note: Mobile QR payment (WeChat Pay/Alipay) is close to essential in Shenzhen — cash is increasingly uncommon; set up a China-compatible payment app before arrival if possible.

data.major_events_note: Chinese New Year and National Day 'Golden Week' are massive nationwide travel surges — domestic transport and hotels book out; best avoided or planned around well in advance.

emergency.notes: 999 covers police, fire, and ambulance. 112 also connects to the same 999 center automatically from any mobile network with coverage.

entry.visa_note: Many Western nationalities (UK, US, EU, Australia, Canada, etc.) can enter visa-free for 30-180 days depending on nationality. Check current requirements by nationality before travel.

transport.payment_card: Octopus card (tap-to-pay, works on MTR, buses, trams, ferries, and many shops)
transport.note: Octopus is the single most useful card for a traveler here — buy at the airport or any MTR station.

data.major_events_note: 17 statutory holidays total in 2026; Lunar New Year and Dragon Boat Festival see the heaviest travel demand and price surges.

entry.visa_note: Most nationalities need an e-Visa or Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) obtained before arrival — India does not offer broad visa-free entry. Apply via the official government e-Visa portal only.
entry.official_source: Bureau of Immigration, Government of India (indianvisaonline.gov.in)

transport.payment_card: Delhi Metro smart card (token also available for single trips)
transport.note: App taxis (Uber/Ola) are generally the safest, most predictable option for tourists over street-hailed vehicles.

data.major_events_note: Diwali and Holi are the two biggest travel-impact festivals — hugely colorful for visitors but also the highest-demand, highest-price periods domestically.

entry.visa_note: Same as Delhi — e-Visa/ETA required in advance for most nationalities.
entry.official_source: Bureau of Immigration, Government of India

transport.payment_card: Mumbai Metro smart card; local trains use separate ticketing
main_systems: Mumbai Suburban Railway (the iconic 'local trains' — extremely crowded at rush hour)
transport.note: Avoid the suburban rail system during rush hour as a first-time visitor — it is famously overcrowded; app taxis are a more comfortable option for short trips.

data.public_holidays_2026: Same national Indian holidays as Delhi, plus regional Maharashtra-specific days (e.g., Gudi Padwa) — confirm regional calendar separately.
data.major_events_note: Ganesh Chaturthi (Aug/Sep, exact date varies) is Mumbai's single biggest cultural event — massive street processions, worth experiencing but expect major crowding and transport disruption.

entry.visa_note: Many Western nationalities visa-free for tourism up to 90 days.
entry.official_source: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan

transport.payment_card: Suica or Pasmo (IC cards, interchangeable, tap-to-pay on virtually all trains/buses/many shops)
transport.note: Tokyo's rail map looks intimidating but Suica/Pasmo works seamlessly across all operators — buy at the airport on arrival.

data.major_events_note: Golden Week (late April-early May) is Japan's biggest domestic travel surge — transport and hotels nationwide book out and prices spike heavily.

emergency.all_purpose: 999 (110 and 112 also redirect to the same 999 center)

entry.visa_note: Visitors from 60+ countries can enter visa-free for 30-180 days depending on nationality. Macau has its own immigration, separate from mainland China and Hong Kong.

transport.payment_card: Macau Pass (contactless card for buses and some shops)
main_systems: Free casino shuttle buses (widely used, connect hotels/attractions)
transport.note: Free hotel/casino shuttle buses are a genuinely useful budget transport hack that locals and savvy tourists both use.

data.major_events_note: Lunar New Year is by far the highest-demand period — casino/hotel prices peak.

entry.visa_note: Visa-free access for 160+ nationalities, typically up to 90 days for tourism.

transport.payment_card: Touch 'n Go eWallet / physical TnG card (covers LRT, MRT, monorail, tolls, and many shops)
transport.note: Avoid unmetered street taxis — stick to Grab or metered taxis for fair pricing.

data.major_events_note: Multiple Islamic and Hindu calendar-based holidays — confirm exact 2026 dates closer to travel.

emergency.general_alt: 911 (also works, especially in the Holy Cities)
emergency.pilgrim_unified_hotline: 1966 (Hajj/Umrah pilgrim support, Saudi SIM) or +966 92 000 2814 (international number)

entry.visa_note: IMPORTANT: entry to Mecca itself is restricted to Muslims only — this is a legal entry restriction, not a custom, and is strictly enforced at checkpoints on roads into the city. Non-Muslim travelers cannot visit Mecca at all. Muslim visitors need either a Hajj visa (Hajj season only, via the official Nusuk platform), an Umrah visa, or can perform Umrah on most other Saudi visa types including the eVisa — but must register on the Nusuk app.
entry.official_source: Nusuk (official Saudi Hajj & Umrah platform) and Saudi eVisa portal (visa.visitsaudi.com)

transport.payment_card: Public transport is limited; most pilgrims move via organized group transport, taxis, or the Al Mashaaer Al Mugaddassah Metro (Hajj-season only, serves the holy sites)
transport.note: Individual/independent tourist-style transport is far less relevant here than in other cities on this list — most visitor movement is organized through pilgrimage service providers.

data.public_holidays_2026: Saudi holidays are Hijri-calendar based (Eid al-Fitr, Eid al-Adha) plus Saudi National Day (Sep 23, fixed Gregorian date) and Founding Day (Feb 22, fixed).
data.major_events_note: This is a fundamentally different kind of entry in this dataset — Mecca is a pilgrimage destination with a hard religious entry restriction, not a general tourism city. The app should flag this distinctly rather than presenting it like a standard destination.

entry.visa_note: Most Western nationalities visa-free for 30-90 days. All travelers must submit the SG Arrival Card electronically before arrival.

transport.payment_card: EZ-Link card or contactless bank card (both work on MRT and buses)
transport.note: Contactless bank card tap-in/tap-out is now fully supported and simplest for short visits — no need to buy a card.

data.major_events_note: Singapore's multicultural holiday calendar means several Islamic and Hindu calendar-based dates shift yearly — always confirm the exact date close to travel.

entry.visa_note: Many Western nationalities visa-free for tourism up to 90 days.
entry.official_source: Taiwan Bureau of Consular Affairs, Ministry of Foreign Affairs

transport.payment_card: EasyCard (tap-to-pay on MRT, buses, and many convenience stores/shops)
main_systems: YouBike (bike share, very popular and EasyCard-compatible)
transport.note: Taiwan uses two separate emergency numbers (110/119), not a single unified one — worth noting clearly since many travelers expect one number.

public_holidays_2026.name: Children's Day / Tomb Sweeping Day cluster

data.major_events_note: Lunar New Year is by far Taiwan's biggest holiday — many small businesses close for several days.

emergency.tourist_police: 1155 (English-speaking, first call for most tourist issues)

entry.visa_note: Many nationalities visa-exempt for tourism (typically 30-60 days depending on nationality). All travelers must complete the Thailand Digital Arrival Card (TDAC) before/on arrival.

transport.payment_card: Rabbit Card (BTS Skytrain) — MRT uses separate token/card system, unification improving
main_systems: Tuk-tuks (short trips only, agree price first)
transport.note: Always insist on the meter in a taxi, or use Grab.

data.major_events_note: Songkran (mid-April) is the single biggest travel disruption/celebration — city-wide water fights, transport and hotel prices spike hard. Confirm exact lunar-calendar holiday dates closer to travel.

entry.visa_note: Same national Thailand rules as Bangkok — visa-exempt for many nationalities (30-60 days), TDAC digital arrival card required for all.

transport.payment_card: No unified transit card — Phuket has no metro/rail system
main_systems: Songthaew (shared pickup trucks, main local transport)
Rented scooters (very common but high accident risk for inexperienced riders)
transport.note: Phuket has no rail transit; getting around relies on songthaews, Grab, or rental scooters — scooter accidents are a leading cause of tourist injury here, wear a helmet and check your travel insurance covers it.

data.public_holidays_2026: Same national Thai holidays as Bangkok.
data.major_events_note: Songkran and the Vegetarian Festival (Phuket-specific, Sep/Oct, exact date varies) are the two biggest local events.

entry.visa_note: Same national Thailand rules as Bangkok/Phuket.

transport.payment_card: No unified card
main_systems: Songthaew (baht buses — main local transport, shared routes)
transport.note: Baht buses run fixed routes along the main roads and are the cheapest way to get around — flag one down and confirm the fare/destination before boarding.

data.public_holidays_2026: Same national Thai holidays as Bangkok.
data.major_events_note: Songkran here is especially large-scale and tourist-oriented compared to more local celebrations elsewhere in Thailand.

emergency.all_purpose: 112 (same unified system as rest of Turkey)

entry.visa_note: Same as Istanbul: e-Visa online in advance for many nationalities, some nationalities visa-free. Turkey is not in the Schengen Area — separate system.

transport.payment_card: Antalyakart (local transit card, separate system from Istanbulkart)
transport.note: Antalya is Turkey's resort/beach hub — most visitors stay in coastal resort areas outside the city center itself.

data.public_holidays_2026: Same national Turkish holidays as Istanbul — see batch 1 Istanbul entry.
data.major_events_note: Peak beach season (Jun-Sep) drives massive resort pricing; this is a summer resort city more than a year-round urban destination.

entry.visa_note: Many nationalities (UK, EU, US, Australia, etc.) receive visa-on-arrival or visa-free entry for 30-90 days.
entry.official_source: The Official Portal of the UAE Government (u.ae)

transport.payment_card: Nol Card (covers Metro, tram, bus, water bus, and RTA taxis)
transport.note: Nol Card is the single card for essentially all public transport in Dubai — buy at any metro station.

data.major_events_note: Most UAE public holidays are Islamic (Hijri) calendar-based and shift ~11 days earlier each year — exact 2026 dates must be confirmed close to travel, official announcement typically comes only weeks ahead.

entry.visa_note: Schengen area — most Western nationalities visa-free for up to 90 days within a 180-day period for tourism/business.
entry.official_source: France-Visas / French Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs

transport.payment_card: Navigo Easy card (rechargeable, works on Metro/bus/RER within zones)
transport.note: Pickpocketing is a real and persistent issue on the Metro and around major tourist sites (especially Gare du Nord) — keep bags zipped and in front.

data.major_events_note: Bastille Day (Jul 14) brings citywide fireworks and a major military parade on the Champs-Élysées — huge crowds, plan transport around road closures.

entry.visa_note: Schengen area — same 90-day visa-free rule (within 180 days) as Paris for most Western nationalities.

transport.payment_card: Roma Pass (includes transit + museum discounts) or standard BIT paper/contactless tickets
main_systems: Metro (2 main lines, limited compared to other major capitals)
transport.note: Rome's Metro network is notably smaller than other major European capitals — buses and walking fill the gaps considerably.

data.major_events_note: Ferragosto (Aug 15) is when much of Rome itself closes as locals leave the city for holiday — many small businesses shut for the surrounding week.

emergency.all_purpose: 112 (unified since 2023 — covers ambulance, police, fire, all emergencies)

entry.visa_note: Many nationalities can obtain Turkey's e-Visa online in advance; some nationalities are visa-free. Check by nationality before travel.
entry.official_source: Republic of Türkiye Electronic Visa Application System (evisa.gov.tr)

transport.payment_card: Istanbulkart (covers metro, tram, bus, funicular, and ferries — cash not accepted on most transport)
transport.note: Istanbulkart is essential — cash payment is not possible on almost any public transport, buy one at the airport immediately.

public_holidays_2026.name: Eid al-Fitr / Ramazan Bayrami (lunar, confirm dates)
Commemoration of Atatürk, Youth and Sports Day
Eid al-Adha / Kurban Bayrami (lunar, confirm dates)

data.major_events_note: Eid holidays (Ramazan and Kurban Bayrami) are the two biggest domestic travel surges — Istanbul empties of some locals but hotel/flight prices still spike due to international demand. Confirm exact 2026 lunar dates closer to travel.

emergency.all_purpose: 999 or 112 (both work identically, free from any phone)

entry.visa_note: Most Western nationalities visa-free for tourism up to 6 months, but the UK's ETA (Electronic Travel Authorisation) is required in advance for many non-visa nationals as of 2025 rules — check current ETA requirement by nationality before travel.

transport.payment_card: Contactless bank card or Visitor Oyster card — contactless is now generally preferred/simpler for short stays
transport.note: Daily fare capping applies automatically with contactless/Oyster — you're never charged more than a day pass equivalent.

data.major_events_note: Notting Hill Carnival (August bank holiday weekend) and Wimbledon (late June/early July) are major tourist-relevant events with big crowd/price impact.

entry.visa_note: Visa Waiver Program covers 40+ countries for tourism up to 90 days, but requires an approved ESTA obtained in advance online. Other nationalities need a B1/B2 visa.
entry.official_source: U.S. Customs and Border Protection / ESTA official site

transport.payment_card: OMNY (tap-to-pay with bank card, phone, or OMNY card — MetroCard fully phased out as of end of 2025)
transport.note: OMNY has weekly fare capping built in — 12 rides in 7 days and the rest of that week is free, so no need to buy a separate weekly pass.

data.major_events_note: Thanksgiving week (including the Macy's Parade) and New Year's Eve in Times Square are the two biggest tourist-impact events — book well ahead and expect major street closures.
